title: Residents revolt against massive AI data center near Philadelphia
excerpt: A crowd of residents gathered at a Pennsylvania middle school auditorium on Wednesday to resist the construction of a massive data center campus.
content_html:
I spent 4 hours at an AI data center town meeting. Things got rowdy.

By Lauren Edmonds

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

2026-07-23T13:06:23.167Z

Read in app

The chair of the planning commission in Upper Merion Township, a suburban town about 20 miles from Philadelphia, began a long-anticipated meeting to discuss a large-scale data center proposal with a warning befitting the middle school auditorium where it was held.

Don't get rowdy, Matt Popek told a packed house. Anyone who disrupts the proceedings will be kicked out. "We recognize there are many strong feelings," he said.

No one was ever asked to leave Wednesday's marathon four-hour meeting, though there were plenty of disruptions. Shouts, boos, applause, chants, and groans filled the room.

"Look at everyone in this room. This is not a divided issue. This is everyone," one public commenter said after asking everyone opposed to stand up. The packed auditorium rose to its feet and roared. "This is an entire community united against hyperscale data centers."

The jeers were directed mostly at Brian O'Neill, the Philadelphia real estate magnate behind MLV Ventures, a venture capital firm, who has proposed building a sprawling AI data center campus in Upper Merion Township that would occupy over 4.5 million square feet across five sites and require between 500 and 900 megawatts of electricity, which would make it one of the largest data centers in the Northeast.

The sheer size, along with environmental concerns and what some consider a lack of transparency around the project, have angered many of the town's residents since they learned about it earlier this year.

Courtney Smith, a member of Upper Merion Against Data Centers, a local activist group, told Business Insider that the development has brought together people of all kinds to resist it.

"If the voice of the community doesn't matter, then where are we? Where are we society-wise?" Smith said. "At some point, the voice of the community has to hold some weight."

Upper Merion Township is one of countless towns across the country resisting the huge data centers required to power the AI products developed by companies like OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Google, Microsoft, and others.

In most of these communities, the opposition is playing out inside townhalls, schools, and community centers, where public planning meetings that typically happen in front of sparse crowds are drawing hundreds. I saw that firsthand on Wednesday in Upper Merion Township.

The meeting was, in a word, testy. It got combative when O'Neill and his team argued with town officials about the project's details — or lack thereof. There was plenty of snark, sarcasm, and a whole lot of finger-pointing.

A packed house

Despite the summer humidity, people began arriving around 5 p.m., an hour before the auditorium doors opened to the public and two hours before the meeting was scheduled to begin. Data center opponents of all ages and backgrounds stood outside the auditorium's entrance, passing out signs and stickers admonishing the proposed development.

One woman I spoke to, Jen Rae, is a longtime resident. She said she lives near one of the proposed sites and is concerned about noise pollution. A common complaint about large data centers is that they can emit a maddening hum.

At first, residents arrived slowly. By 6 p.m., about 35 people were seated in the expansive auditorium, making me wonder whether the pushback against data centers in Upper Merion had fizzled since the last meeting in May.

By the time it began, however, the auditorium was packed.

Applause, boos, and an elusive PowerPoint

It wasn't hard to determine which side the vast majority of attendees took on the issue. When the planning commission called out the developers, the crowd cheered. When the developers spoke, they were heckled.

"Stop with your smirk," one public commenter told O'Neill after she expressed concern about noise levels. "The issue is not that there shouldn't be data centers. There should be data centers. They just shouldn't be built near residential communities."

At the heart of the issue was transparency. Following May's meeting, the planning commission and residents expected the developers to arrive with a detailed breakdown of the data center project. The developers, for their part, said they felt the commission was asking for information not required at this early stage.

When asked, the developers said they would answer all of the residents' questions. Ultimately, though, they didn't.

O'Neill's team said they had submitted a PowerPoint outlining the project details, but the planning commission never displayed it. The planning commission said it couldn't because the developers submitted the PowerPoint "10 minutes" before the meeting began.

That absent PowerPoint became a running punchline throughout the four-hour meeting. When the commission asked for clarification about a detail, the developers would often shrug.

"I don't remember because I don't have our PowerPoint," O'Neill said at one point during a discussion about noise levels, eliciting one of the night's biggest groans from the public. He said that "from memory," the data center would emit about 50 decibels, which he said was "interesting" because it was the equivalent of "a quiet suburb."

The elusive PowerPoint became such a go-to answer that the crowd began anticipating it.

"It's too bad you don't have the slides to back that up," one planning commission member said wryly.

Both residents and the planning commission were frustrated. As a reporter, I understood. I was annoyed by the lack of answers, too.

In the end, following the public comment section, the planning commission announced it would not recommend that the local Board of Supervisors accept the developer's application for the first three locations. The planning commission said it would debate the two final locations in August.

Applause filled the room a final time.

  • Data Centers
  • AI

sentences.zh: 起初，居民们慢慢抵达。

sentences.en: By 6 p.m., about 35 people were seated in the expansive auditorium, making me wonder whether the pushback against data centers in Upper Merion had fizzled since the last meeting in May.
sentences.zh: 到下午 6 点，大约有 35 人坐在宽敞的礼堂里，这让我怀疑自 5 月份的上次会议以来，针对上梅里恩数据中心的抵制是否已经失败。

blocks.html_en: By the time it began, however, the auditorium was packed.

sentences.en: By the time it began, however, the auditorium was packed.
sentences.zh: 然而，当演出开始时，礼堂里已经挤满了人。

sentences.en: A resident opposing the data center speaks during the meeting.
sentences.zh: 一位反对数据中心的居民在会议上发言。

sentences.en: Joe Lamberti for BI
sentences.zh: 乔·兰伯特 (Joe Lamberti) 的 BI

blocks.html_en: Applause, boos, and an elusive PowerPoint

sentences.en: Applause, boos, and an elusive PowerPoint
sentences.zh: 掌声、嘘声和难以捉摸的 PowerPoint

blocks.html_en: It wasn't hard to determine which side the vast majority of attendees took on the issue. When the planning commission called out the developers, the crowd cheered. When the developers spoke, they were heckled.

sentences.en: It wasn't hard to determine which side the vast majority of attendees took on the issue.
sentences.zh: 不难确定绝大多数与会者在这个问题上站在哪一边。

sentences.en: When the planning commission called out the developers, the crowd cheered.
sentences.zh: 当规划委员会点名开发商时，人群欢呼雀跃。

sentences.en: When the developers spoke, they were heckled.
sentences.zh: 当开发商发言时，他们遭到质问。

blocks.html_en: "Stop with your smirk," one public commenter told O'Neill after she expressed concern about noise levels. "The issue is not that there shouldn't be data centers. There should be data centers. They just shouldn't be built near residential communities."

sentences.en: "Stop with your smirk," one public commenter told O'Neill after she expressed concern about noise levels.
sentences.zh: “别再傻笑了，”一位公众评论员在奥尼尔表达了对噪音水平的担忧后告诉她。

sentences.en: "The issue is not that there shouldn't be data centers.
sentences.zh: “问题不在于不应该有数据中心。

sentences.en: There should be data centers.
sentences.zh: 应该有数据中心。

sentences.en: They just shouldn't be built near residential communities."
sentences.zh: 它们不应该建在住宅区附近。”

blocks.html_en: At the heart of the issue was transparency. Following May's meeting, the planning commission and residents expected the developers to arrive with a detailed breakdown of the data center project. The developers, for their part, said they felt the commission was asking for information not required at this early stage.

sentences.en: At the heart of the issue was transparency.
sentences.zh: 问题的核心是透明度。

sentences.en: Following May's meeting, the planning commission and residents expected the developers to arrive with a detailed breakdown of the data center project.
sentences.zh: 五月份的会议之后，规划委员会和居民希望开发商能够带来数据中心项目的详细情况。

sentences.en: The developers, for their part, said they felt the commission was asking for information not required at this early stage.
sentences.zh: 开发商则表示，他们认为委员会正在要求提供早期阶段不需要的信息。

blocks.html_en: When asked, the developers said they would answer all of the residents' questions. Ultimately, though, they didn't.

sentences.en: When asked, the developers said they would answer all of the residents' questions.
sentences.zh: 当被问及时，开发商表示他们会回答居民的所有问题。

sentences.en: Ultimately, though, they didn't.
sentences.zh: 但最终他们没有这么做。

blocks.html_en: O'Neill's team said they had submitted a PowerPoint outlining the project details, but the planning commission never displayed it. The planning commission said it couldn't because the developers submitted the PowerPoint "10 minutes" before the meeting began.

sentences.en: O'Neill's team said they had submitted a PowerPoint outlining the project details, but the planning commission never displayed it.
sentences.zh: 奥尼尔的团队表示，他们已经提交了一份概述项目细节的幻灯片，但规划委员会从未展示过。

sentences.en: The planning commission said it couldn't because the developers submitted the PowerPoint "10 minutes" before the meeting began.
sentences.zh: 规划委员会表示不能，因为开发人员在会议开始前“10 分钟”提交了 PowerPoint。

blocks.html_en: That absent PowerPoint became a running punchline throughout the four-hour meeting. When the commission asked for clarification about a detail, the developers would often shrug.

sentences.en: That absent PowerPoint became a running punchline throughout the four-hour meeting.
sentences.zh: 缺席的 PowerPoint 成为贯穿四个小时的会议的一个亮点。

sentences.en: When the commission asked for clarification about a detail, the developers would often shrug.
sentences.zh: 当委员会要求澄清某个细节时，开发商往往会耸耸肩。

blocks.html_en: "I don't remember because I don't have our PowerPoint," O'Neill said at one point during a discussion about noise levels, eliciting one of the night's biggest groans from the public. He said that "from memory," the data center would emit about 50 decibels, which he said was "interesting" because it was the equivalent of "a quiet suburb."

sentences.en: "I don't remember because I don't have our PowerPoint," O'Neill said at one point during a discussion about noise levels, eliciting one of the night's biggest groans from the public.
sentences.zh: “我不记得了，因为我没有幻灯片，”奥尼尔在一场有关噪音水平的讨论中一度说道，这引发了当晚公众最大的抱怨之一。

sentences.en: He said that "from memory," the data center would emit about 50 decibels, which he said was "interesting" because it was the equivalent of "a quiet suburb."
sentences.zh: 他说，“根据记忆”，数据中心会发出大约 50 分贝的声音，他说这“很有趣”，因为它相当于“一个安静的郊区”。

blocks.html_en: The elusive PowerPoint became such a go-to answer that the crowd began anticipating it.

sentences.en: The elusive PowerPoint became such a go-to answer that the crowd began anticipating it.
sentences.zh: 难以捉摸的 PowerPoint 成为了人们的首选答案，以至于人们开始期待它。

sentences.en: Residents hold signs directed at Brian O'Neill, the real estate developer behind the AI data center proposal in Upper Merion Township.
sentences.zh: 居民举着针对上梅里恩镇人工智能数据中心提案背后的房地产开发商布莱恩·奥尼尔 (Brian O'Neill) 的标语。

sentences.en: Joe Lamberti for BI
sentences.zh: 乔·兰伯特 (Joe Lamberti) 的 BI

blocks.html_en: "It's too bad you don't have the slides to back that up," one planning commission member said wryly.

sentences.en: "It's too bad you don't have the slides to back that up," one planning commission member said wryly.
sentences.zh: “可惜没有幻灯片来支持这一点，”一位规划委员会成员挖苦地说。

blocks.html_en: Both residents and the planning commission were frustrated. As a reporter, I understood. I was annoyed by the lack of answers, too.

sentences.en: Both residents and the planning commission were frustrated.
sentences.zh: 居民和规划委员会都感到沮丧。

sentences.en: As a reporter, I understood.
sentences.zh: 作为一名记者，我理解。

sentences.en: I was annoyed by the lack of answers, too.
sentences.zh: 我也因缺乏答案而感到恼火。

blocks.html_en: In the end, following the public comment section, the planning commission announced it would not recommend that the local Board of Supervisors accept the developer's application for the first three locations. The planning commission said it would debate the two final locations in August.

sentences.en: In the end, following the public comment section, the planning commission announced it would not recommend that the local Board of Supervisors accept the developer's application for the first three locations.
sentences.zh: 最终，在征求公众意见后，规划委员会宣布不建议当地监事会接受开发商前三个地点的申请。

sentences.en: The planning commission said it would debate the two final locations in August.
sentences.zh: 规划委员会表示将在八月就两个最终地点进行辩论。

blocks.html_en: Applause filled the room a final time.

sentences.en: Applause filled the room a final time.
sentences.zh: 房间里最后一次响起掌声。

blocks.html_en: • Data Centers

sentences.en: Data Centers
sentences.zh: 数据中心

sentences.zh: 最受欢迎

blocks.html_en: Lauren Edmonds is an award-winning reporter on the Business News team. When news isn't breaking, she covers personal finance, kitchen-table economics, and paths to financial freedom, including investing, real estate, side hustles, and small business. She also writes about guaranteed and universal basic income programs in the United States.Lauren has also covered lifestyle and entertainment, digital culture, and more. She has a master's degree from the Columbia University Graduate School of Journalism and resides in New York City.Do you have an interesting story to tell? You can reach Lauren at ledmonds@businessinsider.com or on Signal at ledmonds0.07.Popular StoriesNetflix wants to be Disney when it grows up Why Hollywood is paying this 17-year-old up to $20,000 to boost film trailers with TikTok editsHere's all the free money Trump's talked about giving Americans during his second term — and where it all standsA 17-year-old earned $72,000 after investing his e-commerce profits into stocks. Here's why he bet on the tech industry.Lawmakers float a nationwide basic income experiment that would cover the cost of a 2-bedroom apartmentNearly 30,000 Americans have received about $335 million in basic income. Here are 5 takeaways. Americans ditch suffocating healthcare costs and divisive politics to retire in Italy: 'It's the way they approach life'From 'road-schooling' to gas that costs $500, this family of 4 shares what it's like living in a solar-powered Greyhound bus

sentences.en: Lauren Edmonds is an award-winning reporter on the Business News team.
sentences.zh: 劳伦·埃德蒙兹 (Lauren Edmonds) 是《商业新闻》团队中一位屡获殊荣的记者。

sentences.en: When news isn't breaking, she covers personal finance, kitchen-table economics, and paths to financial freedom, including investing, real estate, side hustles, and small business.
sentences.zh: 当没有新闻时，她会报道个人理财、餐桌经济学以及通往财务自由的道路，包括投资、房地产、副业和小企业。

sentences.en: She also writes about guaranteed and universal basic income programs in the United States.Lauren has also covered lifestyle and entertainment, digital culture, and more.
sentences.zh: 她还撰写了有关美国有保障和普遍的基本收入计划的文章。劳伦还报道了生活方式和娱乐、数字文化等。

sentences.en: She has a master's degree from the Columbia University Graduate School of Journalism and resides in New York City.Do you have an interesting story to tell?
sentences.zh: 她拥有哥伦比亚大学新闻研究生院的硕士学位，居住在纽约市。您有什么有趣的故事要讲吗？

sentences.en: You can reach Lauren at ledmonds@businessinsider.com or on Signal at ledmonds0.07.Popular StoriesNetflix wants to be Disney when it grows up Why Hollywood is paying this 17-year-old up to $20,000 to boost film trailers with TikTok editsHere's all the free money Trump's talked about giving Americans during his second term — and where it all standsA 17-year-old earned $72,000 after investing his e-commerce profits into stocks.
sentences.zh: 您可以通过 ledmonds@businessinsider.com 或 Signal 上的 ledmonds0.07 与 Lauren 联系。 热门故事 Netflix 希望长大后成为迪士尼 为什么好莱坞要向这位 17 岁的年轻人支付高达 20,000 美元的费用，通过 TikTok 编辑来宣传电影预告片 以下是特朗普谈到的在第二个任期内向美国人提供的所有免费资金 - 以及这一切的情况 一名 17 岁的年轻人在投资自己的资金后赚了 72,000 美元电商利润转入股票。

sentences.en: Here's why he bet on the tech industry.Lawmakers float a nationwide basic income experiment that would cover the cost of a 2-bedroom apartmentNearly 30,000 Americans have received about $335 million in basic income.
sentences.zh: 这就是他押注科技行业的原因。立法者提出了一项全国性的基本收入实验，该实验将覆盖两居室公寓的费用。近 30,000 名美国人获得了约 3.35 亿美元的基本收入。

sentences.en: Here are 5 takeaways.
sentences.zh: 这里有 5 个要点。

content_html_zh:
我在人工智能数据中心城镇会议上度过了 4 个小时。 事情变得很吵闹。

作者：Lauren Edmonds 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

2026-07-23T13:06:23.167Z

Read in app

距费城约 20 英里的郊区小镇上梅里恩镇 (Upper Merion Township) 的规划委员会主席开始了一场期待已久的会议，讨论一项大型数据中心提案，并发出了适合会议举行地的中学礼堂的警告。

马特·波佩克（Matt Popek）对挤满人的人说，不要吵闹。 任何扰乱程序的人都会被踢出局。 “我们认识到有很多强烈的感情，”他说。

尽管发生了很多干扰，但没有人被要求离开周三长达四小时的马拉松式会议。 喊叫声、嘘声、掌声、口号和呻吟声充满了整个房间。

“看看这个房间里的每个人。 这不是一个分裂的问题。 这就是每个人，”一位公众评论者在要求所有反对者站起来后说道。 挤满了人的礼堂站了起来，欢呼雀跃。 “这是一个团结起来反对超大规模数据中心的整个社区。”

这些嘲笑主要是针对风险投资公司 MLV Ventures 背后的费城房地产大亨布莱恩·奥尼尔 (Brian O'Neill)，他提议在上梅里恩镇 (Upper Merion Township) 建设一个庞大的人工智能数据中心园区，该园区将占地超过 450 万平方英尺，分布在五个地点，需要 500 至 900 兆瓦的电力，这将使其成为东北部最大的数据中心之一。

自从今年早些时候得知该项目以来，庞大的规模、环境问题以及一些人认为该项目缺乏透明度的问题激怒了该镇的许多居民。

当地激进组织 Upper Merion Against Data Centers 的成员考特尼·史密斯 (Courtney Smith) 告诉《商业内幕》，这一开发项目吸引了各类人士共同抵制。

“如果社区的声音不重要，那么我们在哪里？ 我们的社会智慧在哪里？” 史密斯说。 “在某些时候，社区的声音必须具有一定的影响力。”

上梅里恩镇是全国无数拒绝为 OpenAI、Anthropic、Meta、谷歌、微软等公司开发的人工智能产品提供动力所需的大型数据中心的城镇之一。

在大多数社区中，反对派都在市政厅、学校和社区中心内展开，这些地方的公共规划会议通常在稀疏的人群面前举行，吸引了数百人参加。 周三我在上梅里恩镇亲眼目睹了这一点。

总而言之，这次会议很激烈。 当奥尼尔和他的团队与镇官员就该项目的细节（或缺乏细节）争论时，事情变得激烈起来。 其中充满了尖酸刻薄、讽刺和指责。

挤满了人的房子

尽管夏季潮湿，人们还是在下午 5 点左右开始抵达，即礼堂向公众开放前一小时，也是会议预定开始前两小时。 各个年龄段和背景的数据中心反对者站在礼堂入口外，分发标语和贴纸，警告拟议的开发项目。

我采访过的一位女士 Jen Rae 是这里的长期居民。 她说她住在拟建地点之一附近，担心噪音污染。 关于大型数据中心的一个常见抱怨是它们会发出令人发狂的嗡嗡声。

起初，居民们慢慢抵达。 到下午 6 点，大约有 35 人坐在宽敞的礼堂里，这让我怀疑自 5 月份的上次会议以来，针对上梅里恩数据中心的抵制是否已经失败。

然而，当演出开始时，礼堂里已经挤满了人。

掌声、嘘声和难以捉摸的 PowerPoint

不难确定绝大多数与会者在这个问题上站在哪一边。 当规划委员会点名开发商时，人群欢呼雀跃。 当开发商发言时，他们遭到质问。

“别再傻笑了，”一位公众评论员在奥尼尔表达了对噪音水平的担忧后告诉她。 “问题不在于不应该有数据中心。 应该有数据中心。 它们不应该建在住宅区附近。”

问题的核心是透明度。 五月份的会议之后，规划委员会和居民希望开发商能够带来数据中心项目的详细情况。 开发商则表示，他们认为委员会正在要求提供早期阶段不需要的信息。

当被问及时，开发商表示他们会回答居民的所有问题。 但最终他们没有这么做。

奥尼尔的团队表示，他们已经提交了一份概述项目细节的幻灯片，但规划委员会从未展示过。 规划委员会表示不能，因为开发人员在会议开始前“10 分钟”提交了 PowerPoint。

缺席的 PowerPoint 成为贯穿四个小时的会议的一个亮点。 当委员会要求澄清某个细节时，开发商往往会耸耸肩。

“我不记得了，因为我没有幻灯片，”奥尼尔在一场有关噪音水平的讨论中一度说道，这引发了当晚公众最大的抱怨之一。 他说，“根据记忆”，数据中心会发出大约 50 分贝的声音，他说这“很有趣”，因为它相当于“一个安静的郊区”。

难以捉摸的 PowerPoint 成为了人们的首选答案，以至于人们开始期待它。

“可惜没有幻灯片来支持这一点，”一位规划委员会成员挖苦地说。

居民和规划委员会都感到沮丧。 作为一名记者，我理解。 我也因缺乏答案而感到恼火。

最终，在征求公众意见后，规划委员会宣布不建议当地监事会接受开发商前三个地点的申请。 规划委员会表示将在八月就两个最终地点进行辩论。

房间里最后一次响起掌声。

  • 数据中心
  • AI

劳伦·埃德蒙兹 (Lauren Edmonds) 是《商业新闻》团队中一位屡获殊荣的记者。 当没有新闻时，她会报道个人理财、餐桌经济学以及通往财务自由的道路，包括投资、房地产、副业和小企业。 她还撰写了有关美国有保障和普遍的基本收入计划的文章。劳伦还报道了生活方式和娱乐、数字文化等。 她拥有哥伦比亚大学新闻研究生院的硕士学位，居住在纽约市。您有什么有趣的故事要讲吗？ 您可以通过 ledmonds@businessinsider.com 或 Signal 上的 ledmonds0.07 与 Lauren 联系。 热门故事 Netflix 希望长大后成为迪士尼 为什么好莱坞要向这位 17 岁的年轻人支付高达 20,000 美元的费用，通过 TikTok 编辑来宣传电影预告片 以下是特朗普谈到的在第二个任期内向美国人提供的所有免费资金 - 以及这一切的情况 一名 17 岁的年轻人在投资自己的资金后赚了 72,000 美元电商利润转入股票。 这就是他押注科技行业的原因。立法者提出了一项全国性的基本收入实验，该实验将覆盖两居室公寓的费用。近 30,000 名美国人获得了约 3.35 亿美元的基本收入。 这里有 5 个要点。 美国人抛弃令人窒息的医疗费用和分裂的政治，前往意大利退休：“这是他们对待生活的方式”从“道路教育”到花费 500 美元的汽油，这个四口之家分享了住在太阳能灰狗巴士里的感受
